text: How do you start a new project?

options.text: Write a 10-page system architecture doc, set up a monorepo, Docker, CI/CD, and K8s blueprints.
Run create-app, delete all boilerplate, and immediately start hacking business logic.
Open ChatGPT/Claude and type 'build me a full web application for...' and copy paste.
Fire up Neovim, initialize a bare git repo, customize the Makefile, and write raw C/Rust.

text: Your code doesn't work. What is your first move?

options.text: Copy the error, paste it straight into Google/StackOverflow, and copy the first accepted answer.
Launch a debugger, trace the call stack, inspect memory allocation, and verify the complexity.
Ask Claude: 'Why is this failing? Fix it.' and replace the entire file.
Sprinkle console.log('HERE!!!'), console.log(123), and console.log(data) everywhere.

text: What is your stance on writing tests?

options.text: 100% test coverage is the bare minimum. Unit, integration, E2E, and property-based tests are mandatory.
Production is my testing environment. If it compiles (or runs), it ships. Users will tell us.
I tell AI to write my tests, then I disable the failing ones in the CI config.
I write optimal assertions to verify that my algorithm achieves O(N log N) space-time complexity.

text: A designer gives you a mockup. The button is 3px off-alignment. Your action?

options.text: Spend 4 hours writing custom CSS animations, adjusting sub-pixels, and fixing HSL color gradients.
A button is a button. Use default HTML <button>, alignment is a construct of the mind.
Ask AI to generate a 'perfect tailwind glassmorphic button with hover micro-animations'.
I don't do frontend. Command line interfaces don't have buttons.

text: It is 11 PM on a Friday. What are you coding?

options.text: Solving LeetCode Hard problems in C++ to optimize my neural network paths.
Fixing a catastrophic bug in production that I introduced at 4:30 PM.
Re-configuring my Neovim Lua scripts or customizing my keyboard's firmware.
Automating a 5-minute task that took me 6 hours to write an AI script for.

text: What is your absolute favorite development tool?

options.text: Neovim running in a GPU-accelerated terminal, fully navigated via keyboard shortcuts.
Claude / Cursor / GitHub Copilot API.
Chrome DevTools inspector, Figma, and color contrast checkers.
Whiteboard diagramming software, database schemas, and UML generators.

text: How do you name your variables?

options.text: a, b, temp, data2, array_final_v3_really_done.
AbstractFactoryProviderDelegateContextInstanceInitializer.
Descriptive, self-documenting camelCase following strict Clean Code standards.
Whatever the StackOverflow snippet named them, or whatever AI auto-completed.

text: How do you feel about team planning meetings?

options.text: A massive waste of CPU cycles. I could be optimizing memory buffers or sorting lists.
A perfect opportunity to propose migrating our entire system to a Rust-based WASM serverless stack.
Excellent time to keep coding silently with my camera off.
Good! I can showcase my new UI prototypes and custom micro-interactions.
